comment: /** * Possible values for the position of chart tick labels (either horizontal or vertical). * * "none" - does not display the selected tick labels. * * "nextTo" - sets the position of the selected tick labels next to the main label. * * "low" - sets the position of the selected tick labels in the part of the chart with lower values. * * "high" - sets the position of the selected tick labels in the part of the chart with higher values. * @typedef {("none" | "nextTo" | "low" | "high")} TickLabelPosition * **/
description: Possible values for the position of chart tick labels (either horizontal or vertical). * "none" - does not display the selected tick labels. * "nextTo" - sets the position of the selected tick labels next to the main label. * "low" - sets the position of the selected tick labels in the part of the chart with lower values. * "high" - sets the position of the selected tick labels in the part of the chart with higher values.
name: TickLabelPosition

/**

	 * ## Try it
	 * 
	 * ```js document-builder
	 * // Set the axis values to the top position of the chart:
	 * oChart.SetAxieNumFormat("top", "0.00");

comment:
/**
	 * Standard numeric format.
	 *
	 * ## Try it
	 * 
	 * ```js document-builder
	 * // Set the "0.00" numeric format to the chart data point:
	 * oChart.SetDataPointNumFormat("0.00", 0, 1, true);
	 * ```
	 *
	 * @typedef {("General" | "0" | "0.00" | "#,##0" | "#,##0.00" | "0%" | "0.00%" |
	 * "0.00E+00" | "# ?/?" | "# ??/??" | "m/d/yyyy" | "d-mmm-yy" | "d-mmm" | "mmm-yy" | "h:mm AM/PM" |
	 * "h:mm:ss AM/PM" | "h:mm" | "h:mm:ss" | "m/d/yyyy h:mm" | "#,##0_);(#,##0)" | "#,##0_);[Red](#,##0)" | 
	 * "#,##0.00_);(#,##0.00)" | "#,##0.00_);[Red](#,##0.00)" | "mm:ss" | "[h]:mm:ss" | "mm:ss.0" | "##0.0E+0" | "@")} NumFormat
	 */
name: NumFormat

/**
	 * Specifies the part of the range to be pasted.

	 * ## Try it

	 * ```js document-builder

	 * oRange.PasteSpecial("xlPasteValues", "xlPasteSpecialOperationAdd", false, false);
	 * ```
	 *
	 * @typedef {("xlPasteAll" | "xlPasteAllExceptBorders"
	 * | "xlPasteColumnWidths" | "xlPasteComments"
	 * | "xlPasteFormats" | "xlPasteFormulas" | "xlPasteFormulasAndNumberFormats"
	 * | "xlPasteValues" | "xlPasteValuesAndNumberFormats" )} PasteType
	 * */
name: PasteType

/**

	 * ## Try it

	 * // Specifies that the copied data will be added to the data in the destination range of cells:
	 * oRange.PasteSpecial("xlPasteValues", "xlPasteSpecialOperationAdd", false, false);
	 * ```
	 *
	 * @typedef {("xlPasteSpecialOperationAdd" | "xlPasteSpecialOperationDivide" | "xlPasteSpecialOperationMultiply"|
	 * "xlPasteSpecialOperationNone" | "xlPasteSpecialOperationSubtract" )} PasteSpecialOperation
	 * */
name: PasteSpecialOperation

params.description:
A logical value that specifies whether to use the U.S. (NASD) (false or omitted) or European (true) method in the calculation.
According to the European method, the start and end dates that occur on the 31st of a month become equal to the 30th of the same month.
According to the U.S. method, the start date is the last day of a month, it becomes equal to the 30th of the same month.
If the end date is the last day of a month and the start date is earlier than the 30th of a month, the end date becomes equal to the 1st of the next month.
Otherwise the end date becomes equal to the 30th of the same month.
